mappings.rationale: Covers fundamental concepts of income distribution, wealth measurement, and government economic policy.
Directly covers the nature, purposes, and types of taxation including progressive and regressive taxes (subsections B.2 and B.3).
Directly covers income inequality and the distribution of wealth at the bottom of the income spectrum.
Covers how capitalist systems generate patterns of wealth and income distribution through wages, profits, interest, and rent.
Directly covers government-sponsored welfare programs and transfers that affect income distribution (subsection B.4.c).
Covers Marxist critique of wealth distribution under capitalism and the relationship between capital and labour income.
Covers socialist approaches to redistribution of wealth and income through public ownership and planning.
Covers neoliberal approaches to taxation, deregulation, and their effects on income distribution.
Covers Keynesian fiscal policy including budgetary deficits, public debt, and government transfers.
Covers historical patterns of wealth distribution among countries and within populations.
Covers antitrust legislation as a form of direct control over the private sector affecting wealth distribution (subsection B.6.b).
Covers income inequality between developed and developing nations and redistribution mechanisms.
Covers foundational theories of how wealth is distributed through factor shares in the economy.
Covers how global economic integration affects patterns of wealth distribution across and within countries.
Covers labour legislation affecting wage controls and worker welfare programs (subsection B.6.b).
